conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?

Exhausted. I've been working a lot of overtime to get everything done on time, and my social life is basically non-existent. I just want to sit on the couch and watch Netflix without thinking about spreadsheets for five minutes.
You mentioned being exhausted—how has that been affecting your sleep?

I'm getting maybe four or five hours a night. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there, thinking about everything I need to do tomorrow. It's like my brain is still on the clock even when I'm asleep.
You mentioned just wanting to watch Netflix—do you find you're still able to enjoy the shows you watch?
Honestly? No. I'm just watching the same shows I always watch because it’s easy. I'm not really taking it in. I’m just staring at the TV trying to relax.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about everything you need to do, how do you feel about your ability to handle it all?
Like I'm drowning. I keep thinking, 'How am I going to get through this?' And then I just lie there, staring at the ceiling, until I finally fall asleep.
When you're feeling like you're drowning, do you find yourself getting more irritable or on edge with people?
Yeah. I snap at my friends for no reason. I tell them I'm fine, but inside I’m just thinking, 'Why are they still talking when I can barely keep my eyes open?'
You mentioned snapping at your friends—how do you feel about that afterwards?
Guilty. I know I shouldn't take it out on them, but I do. And then I feel like a total jerk, so I just try to make a joke about it and move on.
When you feel like a jerk after snapping at your friends, what kind of thoughts go through your head about yourself?
That I’m losing my grip. That I’m not a good person anymore. That I’m just a burnt-out mess who can't even keep a conversation going without being a total downer.
You said you feel like you're losing your grip—what does that feel like on a day-to-day basis?
It feels like I’m just going through the motions. I show up, I do the work, I go home, and I repeat it all the next day. I’m not really living anymore.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
I don't know, maybe a few months ago? It just kind of... crept up on me. I used to love my job, and now it feels like I’m just a cog in a machine.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. The future, the present, the past. It all just feels empty. I look at my friends, and I see them living their lives, and I'm just... stuck.
